_peak_flops_tf_convention: The values below are the EFFECTIVE per-precision compute baseline used by AC's roofline model, NOT the datasheet peak. They are deliberately set to ~50% of NVIDIA's marketed dense-Tensor-Core peak so that, after the `efficiency_multipliers` block and the per-family efficiency table in throughput_model._DEFAULT_EFFICIENCY_TABLE compose, end-to-end TPS / TBT predictions match published Blackwell early-access traces. See `notes.peak_flops_source` for datasheet numbers. Override via ac-auto-calibrate when lab traces are available.
_datasheet_peak_flops_tf_note: Wave 20 (feedback #2): vendor datasheet dense Tensor-Core peaks, for implied-MFU reporting ONLY. The roofline model must keep using peak_flops_tf. On NVIDIA parts peak_flops_tf is ~half of these (see _peak_flops_tf_convention); on TPU/Trainium the two coincide.

interconnect.topology: NVL72 rack (72-GPU NVLink5 domain) / IB scale-out

system._comment: Rack-scale system layer. The loader maps this block onto the legacy fields: nvlink_domain_size (top-level), interconnect.intra_node_bw_gb_s, interconnect.inter_node_bw_gb_s, gpus_per_rack. Bandwidth units are GB/s (bytes), consistent with every other AC spec. inter_domain 50 GB/s = 400 Gb/s per-GPU ConnectX-7 NDR scale-out port (datasheet figure quoted in Gb/s).

calibration._prefill_multiplier_note: v1-fix demo-audit-2 (Jun 2026): prefill bumped 1.4 -> 2.5 to match published FA-3 throughput on Blackwell, parallel to the H100 fix. Previous calibration reported ~120ms for 7B @ 8k batch=1; FA-3 reaches FP8 efficiency 70-80% on B200, putting the real number at ~50-70ms.

notes.chip_reuse: Single-chip parameters (compute, HBM, SRAM, calibration) are identical to ac/hardware_specs/b200.json — the GB200 NVL72 uses the same B200 GPU silicon; only the system/interconnect layer differs. chip='b200' records this inheritance. NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 product page, https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/gb200-nvl72/ (accessed 2026-07-17, tier T2).
notes.nvlink_domain_size_source: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 product page: 'connects 36 Grace CPUs and 72 Blackwell GPUs in a rack-scale, liquid-cooled design ... a 72-GPU NVIDIA NVLink domain that acts as a single, massive GPU'. https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/gb200-nvl72/ (accessed 2026-07-17, tier T2).
notes.intra_domain_bandwidth_source: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 product page: fifth-generation NVLink provides 1.8 TB/s of GPU-to-GPU interconnect per GPU; NVLink Switch System provides 130 TB/s aggregate for the rack. https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/gb200-nvl72/ (accessed 2026-07-17, tier T2).
notes.inter_domain_bandwidth_source: GB200 NVL72 reference configuration: NVIDIA Quantum-2 NDR InfiniBand via ConnectX-7, 400 Gb/s per GPU scale-out (= 50 GB/s; stored in GB/s per AC convention). Deployment-configurable; 400 Gb/s/GPU is the reference design. NVIDIA partner spec sheets: https://www.spheron.network/gpu-rental/gb200/ and https://pcbstore.com.bd/product/nvidia-gb200-nvl72-48u-rack-solution (accessed 2026-07-17, tier T3).
notes.gpus_per_rack_source: 72 GPUs per NVL72 rack (18 compute trays x 4 GPUs). NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 product page, https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/gb200-nvl72/ (accessed 2026-07-17, tier T2).
notes.peak_flops_source: NVIDIA B200 datasheet (dense, no 2:4 sparsity). BF16 Tensor Core = 2250 TFLOPS; FP8 Tensor Core = 4500 TFLOPS; FP4 Tensor Core = 9000 TFLOPS. The `peak_flops_tf` field above is intentionally ~half of these (see `_peak_flops_tf_convention`). https://nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/dgx-b200/ (accessed 2026-07-17, tier T2).
notes.fused_efficiency_source: FlashAttention-3 benchmarks, FP4 attention newer with lower expected efficiency
notes.hbm_bw_source: NVIDIA B200 datasheet, 8 TB/s HBM3e (192 GB per GPU; 13.4-13.5 TB per NVL72 rack). https://nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/dgx-b200/ (accessed 2026-07-17, tier T2).
notes.compute_units_note: ~160 SMs estimated for B200, verify per SKU (inherited from b200.json).
notes.scope_note: Rack power (~120 kW), cooling, and rack-level failure rates are intentionally NOT modeled (Gate-2 Task C section 1.4 roadmap items).
